text:
was a soldier serving in the Survey Corps in the Attack on Titan: No Regrets manga series.

Appearance

Sairam was a young man with long black hair brushed out of his eyes. He could usually be seen with a serious or angry expression on his face.

Personality

Sairam was a very serious individual, a trait that often put him at odds with the happy-go-lucky Isabel Magnolia. He also proved to be a very brave individual, begging his friend Flagon Turret to save himself rather than try and save him from being devoured by Titans.

Story

Attack on Titan: No Regrets

 
When Isabel Magnolia and Furlan Church begin bickering with each other on their first expedition beyond the Walls, Sairam angrily demands that they take the mission more seriously, reminding them how many soldiers have died in previous expeditions. Before he can get into an altercation with Levi, Flagon Turret calms him down, reminding Sairam that Levi's crew would feel differently about the expedition after seeing a Titan firsthand. When they are attacked by Titans, Sairam and Flagon watch in shock as the three deftly defeat an attacking Titan.

 
When Flagon goes over Erwin's new formation with Levi's group a second time, Sairam is present and gets into an argument with Isabel, questioning if she even understood Erwin's formation. He is pleased with Flagon's optimistic words, but does not appear surprised that Isabel still cannot do a proper salute.

The next day, an unforeseen storm causes the Survey Corps soldiers to get separated, but Sairam manages to stay with Flagon. Later, when they meet Furlan and Isabel, Sairam is surprised to find that Levi is not with them, and wonders aloud if he had been devoured, which angers Isabel who insisted that Levi is still alive. Soon afterward, the four of them are set upon by a group of Titans. Sairam is the first to be devoured, using his final moments to ask Flagon to save himself.

Relationships

Flagon Turret - A friend and his superior, Sairam held an immense amount of respect and loyalty for Flagon, and could usually been seen in his company. Even when being attacked by Titans, Sairam thought only for his friend's safety, encouraging Flagon to save himself.

Isabel Magnolia - Because of her carefree personality and her inexperience as a soldier, the two were constantly at odds, though he could not deny her abilities with vertical equipment. Isabel also showed that, despite their bickering, she respected what he and the rest of the Survey Corps fought for.

Levi - He felt Levi and his friends were a disgrace to the Survey Corps, but like Flagon, he was amazed at his abilities. It is only when Sairam is arguing with Isabel that Levi makes it clear with his body language that, to some degree, Isabel is "off limits."
